title: Kaizen Casa Stone Mortar and Pestle, for Grind Spices, Powder Pesto, Mash Herbs, Crush Pills, Regular Size (3.75"X2.75" Mortar, 5" X1.5" Pestle)
description:
About this item
Adds a trendy touch to any kitchen with stone mortor pestle, good for Grind Spices, Powder Pesto, Mash Herbs, Crush Pills, nice for Birthdays, Christmas, Weddings, or Any Occasion.
Ideal for Nuts, and Seeds to unharness their Full Flavor and Fabulous Aroma, is extra heavy, sturdy and steady, helping it to stay in place on your counter without any shifting or sliding.
It dead grinds something to any texture you want, simply keep pounding till you get your required texture. It’s nice for grinding all of you around the bend, spices, herbs, and leaves.
Mortar and Pestle allows you to release the flavors and oils in freshly ground spices which makes a big difference in the taste of the food. Taste the difference in your favorite recipes.
Size- Mortar 3.75” Dia, 2.75” Deep | Pestle 5" X 1.5”Inches.
reviews_5s: I love it. Does the job and great looking.This is a great mortar and pestle. It’s a good weight and size. You can grind up pills easily. I also use it for herbs and even ground up coarse salt so it would fit in my salt shaker. I find it very useful and easy to clean. It was a great purchase.I've had this product for 2 years now. Great for making Mojitos and grinding herbs and spices. Has held up well over the last two years.I started using this to grind herbs before putting them into cooking. This process really releases a lot of the flavor. This set works really well and is not too heavy.I use this to grind spices for making chai and it works very well. I would say the inside is a bit lighter grey than in the picture but everything else is true to description. I have used it pretty frequently for about a year and it's been a great product.I have had a few mortar & pestle's over the years. I have a big one, but wanted a smaller one for different things that I grind up. This is a very nice one, heavy and sturdy mortar and big pestle, typically bigger than most pestles, which I flat out love. If you're looking for a good mortar & pestle, this is it!!I was so thrilled to get my first mortar and pestle. I am a wildcrafter and an artist. I make my own teas and salves. I also just started making my own earth pigments. I would call this Kaizen Casa Stone set a beginner's set. It is simple and just the right size for small projects, like mine. It washes up really well. I like the feel of it in my hands, though it is not as sturdy with heavier grinds as a more solid, more expensive set. When I first got this I also make gaucamole in it. It does the trick for just what I need.Clearly the color is not the most important factor but after seeing so many reviewers state they received gray instead of black, I’m thrilled that I got a black one which is what I really wanted. The size is perfect for my needs; small amounts of herbs, garlic, etc. It has a lovely modern design and looks perfect in my kitchen. I’m also thrilled that it’s not made in China!I've used a mortar and pestle all my life. For the last 20 years I've had a mortar and pestle that was so big and heavy that I often chose to grind my spices in a coffee grinder. But this kaizen mortar and pestle is the perfect size for my needs.We bought this as a gift for my daughter. She likes to crush of her popery in it. It works really well and is easy to clean up.
reviews_1s:
Play Video
Right out of the box, I noticed a crack-like lining on the mortar (as shown in the video). After using it just three times, the mortar completely broke into two pieces along the same line. This is unacceptable for a product that's supposed to withstand grinding and pounding.

The pestle seemed fine, but without a functional mortar, it’s useless. Disappointing durability and quality. 👎👎I have had this MnP for less than a month and it broke while i was just mixing a small amount of rice and water to season it. I wasn’t muscling it, and the piece just fell off randomly. Scratched my table too :/ bummer because i really liked the size and look of itIt's so soft... why would you make it from this material (cheap)?? Why would I want a fine soapstone powder in everything I grind?? Not practical. Unusable. More Amazon garbage.....It broke the very first time I used it! It broke without doing anything but grinding stevia leaves. I’m outside the return window. So disappointing.It broke the same day I received it as you see I was grinding seeds it broke immediatelyI was grinding some herbs and it broke, used it for just more rhan 7 days. :/When crushing spices this mortar and pestle smears black... paint? stone? into the spices. Whatever it is, that is not how these are supposed to work.Way too smallThe bowl was cracked upon delivey. Not worth your money.Broke the first day
